name: Robot Cell Reach Optimization
description:
A robotics engineer is designing a new manufacturing cell with six workstations arranged in an irregular pattern. Determine the optimal floor mounting location for a robot arm to reach the maximum number of workstation targets.

Target points (in world coordinates, meters):
- WS_1: (0.30, 0.40, 0.30)
- WS_2: (-0.25, 0.50, 0.25)
- WS_3: (0.50, -0.20, 0.35)
- WS_4: (-0.40, -0.30, 0.20)
- WS_5: (0.10, 0.60, 0.40)
- WS_6: (-0.15, -0.55, 0.30)

Connect to the CoppeliaSim simulation (port 23000) and evaluate at least 8 spatially diverse candidate base positions at floor level (Z ~ 0). For each candidate, determine the number of reachable targets (within 0.05m) and the average minimum distance to all targets. Export the detailed results to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/placement_candidates.csv with columns: candidate_id, base_x, base_y, base_z, reachable_count, avg_min_distance_m, and score. Export a summary to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/placement_recommendation.json including total_candidates, best_candidate_id, best_reachable_count, best_base_x, best_base_y, best_base_z, worst_candidate_id, worst_reachable_count, and target_points_evaluated.
